hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W, F, Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212) residues, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L, I, V) will improve steric fit in the SSTR2-specific TM6 pocket (272/273/276/279), thereby increasing selectivity margin over off-targets SSTR3/5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: To achieve higher SSTR2 selectivity, we need to focus on disrupting conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions while preserving the pharmacophore and disulfide bonds.

proposed_changes.rationale: Disrupt conserved pocket interactions at non-pharmacophore positions (1, 2, 5, 6, 11, 12) to reduce off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.
Propose mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt conserved pocket interactions with off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining binding to SSTR2.
